fields.jiraApiTokenPlaceholderSet: Set — leave empty to keep unchanged
fields.telegramBotTokenPlaceholderSet: Set — leave empty to keep unchanged
fields.telegramBotTokenPlaceholderUnset: Not set — global .env token is used

telegram.saveHint: Save changes first — sending uses the saved config.

hints.jiraConnection: Empty = server settings (.env) are used. Fill in if this config needs a different Jira account or instance — e.g. for production use with multiple leads.
hints.telegram: The digest is built from the latest approved sprint: tasks active today on the Gantt, grouped by direction. You can set the bot token here (per-config) — otherwise the global .env token is used. Chat ID is the chat/group id (groups start with -100). The bot must be added to the chat. See the “Help” section for how to create a bot.
hints.telegramNoToken: No bot token is set: neither on this config nor globally in .env. Enter a bot token above (get one from @BotFather) — sending is unavailable without it.
hints.roles: The “In sprint” checkbox enables the role for sprint forming. “Lead” is a flag for auto-adding “Management”.
hints.team: One person — one role. If a person has two roles, create two records with the same accountId.
hints.directions: A direction is determined by Jira labels. Each direction has a pipeline of work types. Developer tasks finishing in the sprint automatically spawn testing (for the analyst) and code review (for the lead). Designer tasks spawn a design review for the lead.
hints.statusBucketMapping: One status can map to different buckets for different roles. For example, «In Progress» = «Development» for a developer and «Testing» for an analyst.
hints.defaultHours: If a task has no estimate, this default is used. For example, for the pair (Development Lead, Code Review) = 1 — the lead spends 1h on review by default.
hints.hoursFields: analyst → Analyst time, tester → Tester time, developer → Developer time, designer → Designer time. Used to estimate hours per bucket.
hints.extraComponents: Project tasks with these components are pulled into the selection regardless of boards.
hints.statusPriorities: The lower the number, the earlier the task gets into the sprint. Global per status.
hints.terminalStatuses: Statuses in which a task is considered done. Used when closing a sprint to calculate completion %.

directions.empty: No directions yet. Add the first one.
directions.designerAutoOption: auto-select (the only one on the team)
directions.pipelineHint: In the “Role” column: a dropdown means you can pin a custom role for this direction instead of the system default; plain text means the role is fixed and can't be overridden.

team.empty: The team is empty. Add someone via “+ Add from Jira”.
team.removeConfirm: Remove this person from the team?

pseudoTasks.noSavedMembers: There are no saved people on the team. First add them via “+ Add from Jira” and save the config.
pseudoTasks.unsavedMembersWarning: There are new people on the team who haven't been saved yet. After saving the config they'll appear in the pseudo-task dropdown.
pseudoTasks.sprintNumTitleRecurring: Sprint number isn't used for recurring tasks
pseudoTasks.sprintNumTitleOnce: Target sprint number (if empty, the task won't be included in any sprint)
pseudoTasks.footnoteRecurring: included in every sprint automatically (for regular tasks like standups).
pseudoTasks.footnoteSprintNum: included only in the sprint with the given number (one-off, e.g. for vacation). If both fields are empty, the task won't be included in any sprint.
pseudoTasks.footnoteManagement: “Management” for leads is added automatically — controlled in the basic settings.
